meta.name: Tex-Mex Chili Con Carne
meta.subtitle: Hearty beef chili with chile-forward flavor, optional beans, and crowd-friendly batch scaling.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Built for weeknight or batch cooking; improves after resting.
Beans are optional to support different chili traditions/preferences.
Designed to pair with tacos, nachos, rice, cornbread, or baked potatoes.

yield.notes: Makes ~10–12 cups depending on simmer time.

summary: Classic Tex-Mex-style chili con carne: browned beef, onion/garlic, chile spice blend, and a long simmer for depth. Optional beans and cocoa/coffee notes for richness.

notes: chile-forward
beefy
savory
slow-simmered

optional.label: Slow cooker (optional finish)
optional.notes: Brown meat/spices first, then slow cook 4–6 hours on LOW.

optional.label: Immersion blender (optional)
optional.notes: Blend a small portion to naturally thicken without flour.

ingredients.label: Ground beef (80/20 preferred)
ingredients.notes: For leaner beef, add a bit more oil for browning.

ingredients.label: Beef chuck, small dice (optional, for chunkier chili)
ingredients.notes: If using chuck, increase simmer time to 2–2.5 hours until tender.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
ingredients.notes: Only needed if meat is very lean.

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Use a blend you like; ancho-style adds depth.

ingredients.label: Diced tomatoes (optional)
ingredients.notes: If you prefer a tomato-forward chili, include. For more chile-forward, reduce or omit.

ingredients.label: Beef broth (or water)
ingredients.notes: Start with 3 cups and add as needed during simmer.

ingredients.label: Unsweetened cocoa powder (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds mole-like depth; do not overdo.

ingredients.label: Strong brewed coffee (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds roastiness; can replace part of the broth.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; broths vary in salt.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar (optional, to finish)
ingredients.notes: Brightens flavors at the end; start small.

steps.title: Prep and measure
instructions: Dice onion (and optional bell pepper). Mince garlic and optional jalapeño.
Measure spices and tomato paste so they’re ready to add quickly.
If using beans, drain and rinse them now.

steps.title: Brown the beef deeply
instructions: Heat a Dutch oven over medium-high. Add neutral oil only if needed (very lean beef).
Add ground beef in an even layer. Let it sear undisturbed 3–4 minutes to develop browning.
Break up and continue cooking until well-browned and most moisture has cooked off.
If there’s excessive grease, spoon off some but leave a little for flavor.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics
instructions: Add diced onion (and optional bell pepper). Cook 4–5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic (and optional jalapeño). Cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Toast the chile-spice blend and tomato paste
instructions: Add chili powder, cumin, oregano, and optional smoked paprika/cayenne.
Stir 30–60 seconds to toast spices.
Add tomato paste and cook 60–90 seconds, stirring, until it darkens slightly.

steps.title: Deglaze and simmer
instructions: Add broth (start with ~3 cups) and scrape up browned bits from the bottom.
If using diced tomatoes, add them now.
Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a low simmer.
Simmer uncovered 40–50 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and flavorful.
Add more broth/water as needed to maintain a thick stew consistency.

steps.title: Optional: add beans and finish simmer
instructions: If using beans, stir them in during the last 10–15 minutes of simmering to heat through.
Taste and adjust salt, chili powder, and cumin as needed.

steps.title: Finish and brighten
instructions: Optional depth: stir in cocoa powder and/or a splash of coffee for extra richness.
Optional brightness: add a small splash of apple cider vinegar (start with 1 tsp).
Rest 10 minutes off heat for best flavor before serving.

notes: Chili tastes better the next day.
Cool quickly; store airtight.
Freeze in portioned containers or freezer bags (lay flat).
Thaw overnight in fridge or reheat gently with a splash of water/broth.
Cool chili promptly (divide into shallow containers for faster cooling).
Reheat until steaming hot before serving.

batchNotes: Double batch fits a large stockpot; increase simmer time to reduce to desired thickness.
For meal prep: portion into 1.5–2 cup containers.
thickeningOptions: Blend 1–2 cups briefly and stir back in for natural thickening.
Mash a portion of beans (if using) and stir back in.
heatControl: Keep cayenne optional; serve hot sauce on the side for mixed preferences.
Use ancho-style chili powder for deep flavor with moderate heat.

notes: Omit beans; increase beef by 0.5–1 lb if you want more volume.
Simmer a bit longer to build body.
Use a mix of kidney + pinto (or black).
Add beans only near the end to prevent them from breaking down too much.
Brown chuck cubes first, then proceed. Simmer 2–2.5 hours until tender.
Add extra broth as needed; longer simmer increases reduction.
Use smoked paprika and a small amount of chipotle powder.
Optionally add a spoon of chipotle sauce (see chipotle_sauce.json) at the end.
